summary:

The decline, driven by weak sentiment, sharemarket losses, and higher interest ra

sources.title: Australian economy loses momentum as leading index falls in March
sources.excerpt:
Australia’s Leading Index slipped to -0.13 per cent in March from 0.05 per cent, signalling below-trend growth ahead. 
The decline, driven by weak sentiment, sharemarket losses, and higher interest rates, reflects a 0.44-point drop since October. 
While commodity prices and approvals offered support, inflation risks persist, with the Reserve Bank of Australia likely to raise rates by 25 bps in May.

Core inflation eased to 1.9 per cent, while s

sources.title: Italy's inflation edges up to 1.7% in March: Istat
sources.excerpt:
Italy’s inflation rose to 1.7 per cent year on year in March 2026, driven by higher energy, according to Istat.  Monthly inflation stood at 0.5 per cent. 
Core inflation eased to 1.9 per cent, while services inflation slowed. 
The HICP increased 1.6 per cent annually, with lower-income households experiencing relatively smaller price rises than higher-spending groups.

However, February imports rose 10.9 per cent YoY on imp

sources.title: Philippines textile imports decline, February signals demand uptick
sources.excerpt:
Philippines’ textile and apparel imports fell 4.3 per cent in Jan–Feb 2026 to $371.81 million, weighed by lower yarn, fabric, and fibre inflows.
However, February imports rose 10.9 per cent YoY on improved demand.
Exports strengthened up 19.7 per cent in February and 6.1 per cent in the two-month period, signalling steady trade recovery trends.

title: Burkina Faso fully nationalises leading cotton firm Sofitex

Sofitex was a mixed-ownership firm,

sources.title: Burkina Faso fully nationalises leading cotton firm Sofitex
sources.excerpt:
Burkina Faso has announced the full nationalisation of Burkinabe Company of Textile Fibres (Sofitex), citing rising debt, declining production and inefficiencies. 
Sofitex was a mixed-ownership firm, in which the state held a majority stake. 
Full state ownership is expected to lead to tighter financial discipline, improved governance and a restructuring of operations to boost efficiency.

title: US AAFA urges CARB to align with existing emissions reporting outline
summary: AAFA, responding to the California Air Resources Board's (CARB) request for feedback on GHG emissions reporting requirements under SB 253, urged CARB to align with existing emissions reporting framewo

sources.excerpt:
AAFA, responding to the California Air Resources Board's (CARB) request for feedback on GHG emissions reporting requirements under SB 253, urged CARB to align with existing emissions reporting frameworks and ensure flexibility in the use of standards. 
AAFA recommended that CARB not mandate specific methods and allow flexibility to report using whichever method is the best fit for the reporting entity.

title: UK’s clothing exports down 7.6% to $293 mn in Jan-Feb
summary:

Textile and fibre exports also fell year on year, though m

sources.title: UK’s clothing exports down 7.6% to $293 mn in Jan-Feb
sources.excerpt:
UK's clothing exports declined 7.66 per cent to £217 million (~$293 million) in February 2026, amid weak European demand and cost pressures. 
Textile and fibre exports also fell year on year, though month-on-month trends show mild recovery. 
Full-year 2025 exports remained under pressure, extending a post-pandemic correction. 
Q4 data indicates only a partial sequential rebound.

title: UK’s apparel imports increase 2.3% to $1.78 bn in Feb 2026
summary:
The UK’s clothing imports rose 2.32 per cent year on year (YoY) to £1.320 billion (~$1.789 billion) in February 2026, with a marginal month on month (MoM) increase. 
Fabric imports edged up, while fib

sources.title: UK’s apparel imports increase 2.3% to $1.78 bn in Feb 2026
sources.excerpt:
The UK’s clothing imports rose 2.32 per cent year on year (YoY) to £1.320 billion (~$1.789 billion) in February 2026, with a marginal month on month (MoM) increase. 
Fabric imports edged up, while fibre imports remained flat. 
Despite softer quarterly trends in late 2025, full-year 2025 apparel imports grew over 6 per cent, signalling resilient demand amid mixed textile segment performance.

title: Italy’s OVS’ FY25 sales rise 7% to $2.06 bn; beats market

Adjusted gross margin rose 8.8 per cent, while

sources.title: Italy’s OVS’ FY25 sales rise 7% to $2.06 bn; beats market
sources.excerpt:
OVS has posted record FY25 sales of €1,745.9 million (~$2.06 billion), up 7 per cent YoY, driven by like-for-like growth and Goldenpoint consolidation. 
Adjusted gross margin rose 8.8 per cent, while net profit increased 14.8 per cent. 
Key brands delivered solid EBITDA gains. 
Womenswear and beauty led growth, with early FY26 performance remaining strong on robust collection demand.

title: Revoking China PNTR may lead to higher tariffs borne by US firms: AAFA
summary: US trade body AAFA has urged the International Trade Commission not to revoke the permanent normal trade relations (PNTR) status granted to China as that would result in higher tariffs borne by US com

sources.title: Revoking China PNTR may lead to higher tariffs borne by US firms: AAFA
sources.excerpt:
US trade body AAFA has urged the International Trade Commission not to revoke the permanent normal trade relations (PNTR) status granted to China as that would result in higher tariffs borne by US companies. 
Higher tariffs on Chinese imports would constrain US firms’ ability to invest in innovation, expand operations and support US job growth, and would risk closing off commercial opportunities in China.

title: India’s T&A exports fall 14% in March, FY26 down 2.21%
summary:

Apparels dropped 18.99 per cent and textile

sources.title: India’s T&A exports fall 14% in March, FY26 down 2.21%
sources.excerpt:
India’s textile and apparel exports fell sharply in March 2026, declining 14.02 per cent year on year (YoY) to $2.91 billion amid geopolitical disruptions. 
Apparels dropped 18.99 per cent and textiles 9.91 per cent. 
FY 2025–26 exports slipped 2.21 per cent, with sector share easing to 8.10 per cent. 
Cotton imports surged annually, while MMF trends remained relatively stable.

title: US CBP to soon launch electronic system for importers to claim refunds
summary:
US Customs and Border Protection will launch on April 20 an electronic system that importers can use to claim tariff refunds authorised by court order or applicable law. 
Phase 1 will be limited to ce

sources.title: US CBP to soon launch electronic system for importers to claim refunds
sources.excerpt:
US Customs and Border Protection will launch on April 20 an electronic system that importers can use to claim tariff refunds authorised by court order or applicable law. 
Phase 1 will be limited to certain unliquidated entries and certain entries within 80 days of liquidation.
Refunds will be issued within 60-90 days of the Consolidated Administration and Processing of Entries declaration getting accepted.

sources.title: Bangladesh RMG units call for allowing local FOC raw material sourcing

Industry leaders said allowing FOC sourcing from domestic suppliers would boost local sales, strengthen backward linkage industries and raise overall value addition in the export-oriented sector. 
Many local suppliers can provide inputs now against global orders.
